tactic: Vibe-coded flight simulator (fly.pieter.com) hit $0 to $1M ARR in 17 days. Built in 3 hours with Cursor. Monetized via in-game ad blimps ($1K each) + F16 plane sales ($29.99). 320K players. Free-to-play browser MMO.
reviewer_notes: Verified revenue via public tweets. Model: vibe-code a game in hours then monetize via virtual goods + sponsor ad placements. Replicable pattern for any niche game.
extracted_method: Build browser games with AI coding tools (Cursor/Claude Code) in hours. Monetize with virtual goods ($29.99 planes) + sponsor blimps ($1K each). Free-to-play drives volume.

tactic: X/Twitter creator program paying $11.5K/mo: $7870 ad rev share + $2683 X subs revenue. Record month. Consistent $9-11K range across multiple months.
reviewer_notes: Verified across multiple monthly reports. Requires large following (600K+). Still shows X monetization is real and scaling. Good benchmark for content accounts.
extracted_method: Build X following to 100K+. Apply for X creator program. Post consistently (daily). Revenue scales with impressions. Ad rev share + subscription model stacks.

tactic: $420K/mo revenue portfolio breakdown: PhotoAI $161K + ReadMAKE $93K + Nomads $61K + InteriorAI $43K + merch/X $34K + RemoteOK $29K. 80% profit margin. Zero employees.
reviewer_notes: Peak was post-Lex Fridman appearance (2.5x spike). Normal run rate ~$140K/mo. Still proves: portfolio of 6 small products > 1 big product. 80% margins because solo + minimal infra.
extracted_method: Portfolio model: 5-7 products each earning $20-60K/mo. Solo operation. Shared VPS infrastructure ($500/mo total). AI products have 99%+ margins (GPU cost ~$200/mo for 21K designs). Scale via media appearances.

tactic: Interior AI: 99%+ profit margins. GPU bill $200/mo for 21000 designs/mo = $0.01/render. Hosted on shared VPS ~$50/mo. 1000+ recurring customers at $40K MRR.
reviewer_notes: Verified cost structure. Key insight: AI image generation is nearly free at scale. Use open-source models on cheap GPUs. Shared infrastructure across products slashes per-product cost to near zero.
extracted_method: Build AI wrapper tool for specific niche (interior design). Open-source SD models keep GPU costs at $0.01/render. Shared VPS across products. Subscription model for recurring revenue. 99% margins achievable.

tactic: 20 mega shifts making 2026 best startup era in 30 years: (1) Open-source AI on cheap robots (2) SaaS imploding as AI replicates $500K software for pennies (3) Outcome-based pricing eating subscriptions (4) Claude Code killed engineering bottleneck (5) App recombination era.
reviewer_notes: Greg sold 3 startups and backed seed-to-unicorn companies. These are directional signals not tactics. Key actionable: build vertical AI workflows by recombining 3-4 existing AI tools. Outcome-based pricing = new SaaS model.
extracted_method: Build vertical AI tools by recombining existing AI APIs. Price on outcomes not subscriptions. Use Claude Code/Cursor to ship MVP same day. Target specific industry workflows (legal/finance/marketing).

tactic: How to build mobile apps with AI in 2026: (1) Claude Code/Rork/Vibecode for MVP same day (2) Claude Code for edge cases (3) Design core interaction for 10-second screen recording (4) Use short-form video as live feedback channel (5) Ship-demo-observe-tighten-charge-repeat.
reviewer_notes: Specific 31-point framework. Key insight: paste TikTok/Reels comments into Claude Code to cluster feedback into product changes. Demo video IS the marketing. Ship smallest change that makes demo clearer then re-record same day.
extracted_method: MVP same day with Claude Code/Rork/Vibecode. Record 10-sec demo. Post to short-form platforms. Read comments. Paste into Claude Code for product iteration. Re-record. Charge when momentum compounds. Video = feedback channel not just marketing.

tactic: What's coming: (1) AI kills 9-5 for millions (2) Livestreaming explodes 100x as only way to prove you're real (3) Portfolio of agent-assisted micro-businesses replaces jobs (4) Most valuable 2026 skill: knowing when NOT to use AI. Human touch trades at 20x premium.
reviewer_notes: Directional signal. Actionable: (a) Build tools for the 'forced freelancer' wave (b) Livestreaming infrastructure/tools (c) Position human-touch services at premium pricing (d) AI agent portfolio management tools.
extracted_method: Position for forced-freelancer wave. Build agent-assisted micro-business tools. Invest in livestreaming (proof-of-human). Offer human-touch premium services alongside AI automation. Portfolio of small AI apps model.

tactic: 2026 AI predictions: (1) SaaS and agents merge completely (2) Google crushes it while OpenAI pivots social (3) AI companions $50B market (4) Voice-first interfaces dominate (5) Micro-companies of 1-3 people replace traditional businesses.
reviewer_notes: 43 total predictions. Key actionable: every SaaS must become agent platform. AI companion apps are massive (check app store rankings at 2am). Voice interfaces = next UI paradigm. Micro-company tools = infrastructure play.
extracted_method: Build SaaS with agent capabilities built in. AI companion/relationship apps = $50B TAM. Voice-first interfaces for specific verticals. Tools for micro-companies (1-3 person businesses running AI agents).

tactic: SaaS stock correction coming. AI productivity gains lead to 100K+ SaaS layoffs in 2026. Laid-off workers become founders. 'Portfolio of apps' becomes common phrase. Massive supply of technical talent entering indie hacking.
reviewer_notes: Directional signal. Actionable: (a) Build for the wave of new founders (tools/templates/courses) (b) Hire from the talent surplus (c) Position now before competition floods in (d) Info products teaching 'portfolio of apps' model.
extracted_method: Build tools/templates for incoming wave of laid-off-turned-founders. Info products on 'portfolio of apps' model. Hire technical talent at discount during surplus. Move fast before flood of new competitors.

tactic: YC Spring 2026 Request for Startups breakdown: (1) Cursor for product managers (AI decides what to build not just how) (2) AI-native systems synthesizing customer feedback into product requirements (3) Agents that ship features autonomously.
reviewer_notes: YC signals = where VC money flows. Cursor-for-X architecture = default for knowledge work. Build AI PM tools that take customer calls + usage data and output 'build this next' + tasks. Vertical applications of this pattern.
extracted_method: Build 'Cursor for X' tools for non-engineering knowledge work (legal/finance/marketing/sales/ops). AI PM tool that ingests customer feedback and outputs prioritized features + agent-executable tasks.

tactic: Day 375 growing SaaS to $1M. 40% MoM growth. $94.8K/mo across portfolio: CodeFast $73.8K + ShipFast $31.5K + IndiePage $2K + DataFast $1.7K + 7 other micro-products. Strategy: launch many small products and share results publicly.
reviewer_notes: Feb 2025 revenue breakdown verified via public tweet. CodeFast is an info product (coding course) not SaaS. Key insight: info products 10x easier to sell than SaaS. Portfolio of 12 products diversifies risk. 40% MoM growth on flagship.
extracted_method: Portfolio approach: 12 micro-products. Info products (courses) outsell SaaS 10x. Share revenue publicly to build audience. Use audience to launch next product. Flywheel: launch-share-grow-launch.

tactic: Grew Twitter to 170K followers with 3 steps: (1) Launch tiny startups (2) Share results (3) Repeat. Built DataFast analytics tool to share your metrics with a public link. Audience grows when you start winning publicly.
reviewer_notes: Verified follower count. Simple but effective: build-in-public as distribution strategy. DataFast tool makes sharing analytics frictionless. Each product launch = content event = follower growth = next product audience.
extracted_method: Build in public. Every product launch = content event. Share real numbers (revenue/users/growth). Build tools that make sharing results frictionless (DataFast-style). Audience from Product A becomes customers for Product B.

tactic: Info products 10x easier to sell than SaaS. CodeFast course made $92K in 2 days at launch. 3 reasons: (1) Universal problem (everyone wants to learn to code) (2) Lots of social proof from building in public (3) Built-in audience from prior products.
reviewer_notes: Verified $92K in 2 days. Key: course priced at $169. Universal pain point + existing audience + social proof = explosive launch. Replicable for any skill where you have proven results.
extracted_method: Info products > SaaS for initial revenue. Price at $99-199 range. Target universal problems. Build audience first via building in public. Use social proof from real products. Launch to existing audience for explosive day-1 revenue.

tactic: Sold Small Bets community to Gumroad for $3.6M. Deal: 50% cash ($1.8M) + 50% stock options. Community had 6692 lifetime members. Vassallo continues running it as autonomous subsidiary. Open-sourced the Campfire platform.
reviewer_notes: Verified via multiple sources. Key insight: communities are acquirable assets. $3.6M / 6692 members = $537/member lifetime value. Build community + course + open-source platform = triple asset value. Gumroad acquiring communities to become education platform.
extracted_method: Build paid community around a methodology (small bets/portfolio approach). Open-source the platform to add enterprise value. Position for acquisition by larger platform. $537/member = benchmark for community valuation.

tactic: Small Bets platform open-sourced (modified Campfire). GitHub bounties for contributors. Community-driven development. Strategy: open-source your platform adds enterprise value and creates ecosystem around your brand.
reviewer_notes: Open-source strategy contributed to acquisition value. Campfire fork = purpose-built community platform. Bounties attract developers. Open-source creates lock-in paradoxically because community builds on your platform.
extracted_method: Open-source your community platform. Use GitHub bounties to attract contributors. Modified Campfire = custom community experience. Open-source adds enterprise value for potential acquirers.

tactic: 2026 Cold Email Benchmarks from billions of emails analyzed: avg reply rate 3.43%. Top performers >10%. Top quartile 5.5%. Key success factors: micro-segmentation + problem-focused messaging + frequent A/B testing + smart automation (auto-triaging replies + auto-scheduling follow-ups).
reviewer_notes: Industry benchmark report from Instantly (major cold email platform). Data from billions of emails. Actionable: target 5.5%+ reply rate. Must use micro-segmentation. Problem-first messaging. A/B test constantly. Auto-triage replies.
extracted_method: Cold email 2026: start new domains at 5-10 emails/day. Ramp over 4-6 weeks. Micro-segment lists. Problem-first messaging under 80 words. Single CTA. Frequent A/B testing. Auto-triage replies. Target 5.5% reply rate (top quartile).

tactic: The Baking Method: year-long cold email follow-up sequence. Automated 12-month nurture. Key insight: most deals close in follow-up months 3-12 not initial send. Prevents getting ghosted. Proven across manufacturing to tech services.
reviewer_notes: Alex Berman has 100K+ YouTube subscribers and 14K+ agencies trained. Method sold at $497. Key: most cold emailers give up after 3-5 follow-ups. 12-month sequence captures deals competitors abandon. Automation makes this zero-effort after setup.
quality_issues: Selling to cold email audience so method claims need verification
extracted_method: Build 12-month automated follow-up sequence. Most deals close months 3-12 not on first email. Use Instantly/Smartlead for automation. Set up once then forget. Captures deals competitors abandon after week 2.

tactic: Ship 30 for 30: daily writing challenge turned into $5M+ business. Started at $50/cohort in 2021 now $999/cohort ($1999 premium). 326K Twitter followers in 30 months. Framework: everything starts as tweet then expand to atomic essay then long-form.
reviewer_notes: $5M from single digital product verified via multiple interviews. Pricing evolution from $50 to $999 = 20x increase as audience grew. Key: cohort model creates urgency + community + accountability. Lean writing system: tweet-first then expand.
quality_issues: Selling course about making money from courses. Earnings claim HIGH but verified via multiple sources
extracted_method: Cohort-based writing course model. Start at low price ($50) build social proof then raise (now $999). Everything starts as tweet. Lean writing: tweet > atomic essay > long-form. Daily publishing builds audience. 326K followers in 30 months via consistent daily shipping.

tactic: SuperPrompt: open-source XML agent prompt that makes Claude generate novel ideas. Uses XML tags + mathematical/philosophical concepts to explore areas of the model usually unexplored. Forces out-of-the-box thinking via structured prompt engineering.
reviewer_notes: GitHub repo (NeoVertex1/SuperPrompt). Technical tool for prompt engineering. Can be used to generate novel product ideas or content angles. Works best with Claude. Practical application: use as system prompt for brainstorming sessions.
extracted_method: Use SuperPrompt (open-source XML agent) as system prompt for Claude brainstorming sessions. Forces LLM into novel reasoning patterns. Apply to: product ideation + content angle generation + problem-solving. Free tool on GitHub.

tactic: 2026 sector calls: (1) Solar energy stocks up 50-60% already (2) Testing and packaging equipment suppliers up (3) Google CAPEX $111B in 2026 (+29% YoY) (4) DRAM demand from OpenAI 900K WPM by 2029 (5) SanDisk won Google eSSD contract ramping 1H 2026.
reviewer_notes: Technical semiconductor analysis. Not directly actionable for solopreneur ops but relevant for: (a) investment portfolio allocation (b) understanding AI infrastructure buildout trajectory (c) content creation for finance niche accounts.
extracted_method: Sector investing signals: solar energy + testing/packaging equipment + DRAM suppliers benefiting from AI infrastructure buildout. Google CAPEX $111B in 2026. Use for stock/crypto content creation and personal portfolio allocation.

tactic: Comfyflows marketplace for ComfyUI workflows. Built and launched in 26 hours. Features: find/share/sell workflows + run as API. ComfyUI rebuilt their Python pipeline to be 2x faster. Marketplace model = other people create the product you just provide the platform.
reviewer_notes: Danny Postma (HeadshotPro founder) built marketplace in 26 hours. Key insight: marketplace model where community creates content/workflows and you take a cut. ComfyUI is dominant open-source AI image pipeline. Workflow marketplace = sell pickaxes in gold rush.
extracted_method: Build marketplace for AI tool workflows/templates (ComfyUI/n8n/Make). 26-hour build. Community creates the product. You take platform cut. Sell pickaxes in AI gold rush. Target: creators who want to monetize their workflows.

tactic: Transition from solopreneur to team. Key learning: shadowing other remote team structures before hiring. Scaling from solo to team is the hardest transition. HeadshotPro growth required hiring but the mindset shift is what matters most.
reviewer_notes: Honest about difficulties scaling. Not a tactical framework but a directional signal: when to hire and how to transition. Relevant for PRINTMAXX when scaling past solo capacity.
extracted_method: When scaling past solo capacity: shadow other remote teams before hiring your own. Study their structures. Mindset shift matters more than process. Start with contractors before full-time.

tactic: God of Prompt platform: curated prompt engineering products with lifetime updates. Structured prompt engineering boosts AI model accuracy by up to 25%. Key prediction: 60% of Fortune 500 will integrate prompt engineering into AI strategies by 2026.
reviewer_notes: Platform selling prompt bundles. 25% accuracy improvement stat from blockchain.news (needs verification). Actionable: prompt engineering products (templates/guides/courses) remain viable digital product category. Enterprise demand growing.
quality_issues: Quality of 25% claim unclear - sourced from blockchain.news aggregator
extracted_method: Sell prompt engineering products (bundles/guides/templates). Enterprise demand growing. Structured prompts boost accuracy ~25%. Package as Gumroad digital products at $27-97 range. Lifetime updates model creates recurring value perception.

tactic: Sam Parr model: sold The Hustle to HubSpot. Now co-founded Hampton (high-end entrepreneur community). My First Million podcast drives deal flow. Pattern: media company > exit > high-ticket community > podcast flywheel.
reviewer_notes: Verified exit (HubSpot acquisition of The Hustle). Hampton = vetted membership community for entrepreneurs/founders/CEOs. Pattern: build audience via newsletter > exit > reinvest into premium community > use podcast for deal flow and authority.
extracted_method: Newsletter-to-exit pipeline: build newsletter audience > monetize > exit to larger company > reinvest into premium community ($5K+/yr membership). Use podcast as deal flow + authority engine. Media company = startup with built-in distribution.

tactic: ChatGPT 4o image gen is as big as the ChatGPT launch itself. Will birth 1000+ businesses doing $1-100M/year in vertical software. We're in the 'MP3 Napster era' of content. Millions of creators don't realize their back catalog is being consumed by AI.
reviewer_notes: Two distinct insights: (1) Image gen = massive vertical software opportunity (build niche image tools like levelsio's InteriorAI) (2) Creator content being consumed by AI = new rights/licensing business opportunity. Both are HIGHEST ROI.
extracted_method: Build vertical image generation tools for specific niches (interior design/real estate/fashion/food). ChatGPT 4o image gen = platform shift. Also: creator content licensing/protection tools = emerging market. 1000+ $1-100M businesses to be built.
